macro_segments.title: Introduction to Mashiach and the Fallen Dynasty: Context from Sancheriv and Nevuchadnezzar

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks connecting the fall of empires to the question of when Mashiach will come
Rav Nachman's dialogue with Rav Yitzchak: calling Mashiach 'Bar Nafli' (son of the fallen)
Rabbi Yochanan's teaching on the generation when Mashiach comes: diminished sages, afflictions, and continuous tragedies

macro_segments.title: The Seven-Year Shmita Cycle Before Mashiach: Famine, Wars, and Renewal

micro_segments.title: Tanu Rabbanan: detailed description of the seven-year cycle leading to Mashiach's arrival, year by year
Rav Yosef's challenge: the predicted sequence did not actually occur in history
Abaye's response: the events did not happen in order or as predicted

macro_segments.title: The Generation of Mashiach: Moral and Social Collapse According to Rabbi Yehuda

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yehuda's description: houses of assembly become brothels, the Galilee destroyed, border peoples unsettled
Continuation: wisdom of sages spoiled, those who fear sin despised, face of generation like a dog
The loss of truth: exposition of 'truth will be absent' and the story of the city called Truth
The story of Rava: the man from the city of Truth who lied to protect his wife's privacy and lost his sons

macro_segments.title: Alternative Accounts of the Generation of Mashiach: Rabbi Nehorai and Rabbi Nechemiah

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Nehorai: youth disrespect elders, children disrespect parents, no shame before fathers
Rabbi Nechemiah: brazenness increases, honor perverted, wine plentiful but expensive, entire kingdom turns to heresy
Support from Rabbi Yitzchak: the heresy conversion as a necessary sign; the metaphor of complete whitening of leprosy

macro_segments.title: Conditions for Mashiach's Arrival: Loss of Hope and Divine Judgment

micro_segments.title: Interpretation of 'God will judge His people': multiple derivations from the verse about loss of power and support
Four possible meanings of 'His hand is extended': increased informants, decreased sages, no money, or despair of redemption

macro_segments.title: The Danger of Computing the End: Rabbi Zeira's Warning and the Three Unbidden Things

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Zeira's teaching: do not push away the time of Mashiach by trying to compute when he will come
The Braita: three things come if you do not pay attention—Mashiach, found object, and scorpion

macro_segments.title: Cosmic Timeline: The Six Thousand Year Lifespan of the World

micro_segments.title: Rav Katina: the world has a 6,000-year lifespan with a thousand-year sabbath at the end; Abaye's variant of 2,000 years
The analogy of the Shmita year: just as the seventh year is fallow, so the world rests in the seventh millennium
Tana d'vei Eliyahu: dividing the 6,000 years into three 2,000-year epochs—tohu, Torah, and Yemot HaMashiach

macro_segments.title: Specific Computations and Prophecies: The Scroll from Rome and the Year 4291

micro_segments.title: Rav Chana bar Tachlipa's account: a scroll found in Roman storehouses predicting the world's end after 4,291 years
Wars of sea creatures and Gog u'Magog followed by the rest of the period of Mashiach
Statement that God does not renew the world until after seven thousand years
Rav Acha bar Rava's alternative: after 5,000 years, with conflicts and renewal timing

macro_segments.title: The Impossibility of Computation: Rabbi Nasan's Piercing Verse and the Fate of Calculators

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Nasan: the verse 'the vision is yet for its appointed time' pierces down to the depths, rejecting all computations
Rejection of three rabbinic interpretations: the עידן עידנין calculation, Rabbi Simlai's bread and tears, Rabbi Akiva's early expectation
The three periods of Jewish kingship: 70 years (Hasmonean), 52 years (Herod), and 2.5 years (Bar Kokhba)

macro_segments.title: Cursing Those Who Compute and the Divine Waiting: Tiphach and Mercy

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Shmuel bar Nachmani in the name of Rabbi Yonasan: may the bones of those who compute the end be blasted
Why they fail: those who compute set a date, Mashiach does not come, they despair that he will never come
The solution: simply wait for Mashiach without trying to compute; God also waits to show mercy
Who prevents Mashiach's coming: the Attribute of Justice; we must earn redemption through deeds and repentance
The reward for waiting: those who wait for Mashiach receive blessing; this is the meaning of the thirty-six righteous

macro_segments.title: The Thirty-Six Righteous in Every Generation and Their Relationship to the Divine Presence

micro_segments.title: Abaye's statement: the world has no fewer than thirty-six righteous who receive the Divine Presence in every generation
Gematria proof: the word 'him' (לו) has numerical value 36
Objection from Rava: does this not contradict Rava's statement about eighteen thousand parasang distances?
Resolution: the thirty-six see God through a clear lens; the eighteen thousand are those who see through a cloudy lens
Objection from Chizkiyah quoting Shimon bar Yochai: the righteous of stature are very few, perhaps only two

macro_segments.title: The Debate Between Rabbi Eliezer and Rabbi Yehoshua: Does Mashiach Come Through Repentance or Divine Decree?

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Eliezer: if Israel does repentance, they will be redeemed; if not, they will not be redeemed
Rabbi Yehoshua's first response: God will raise up an evil king like Haman, forcing Israel to repent and improve
Second debate (Tanya Idach): Rabbi Eliezer quotes 'return wayward children'; Rabbi Yehoshua quotes 'sold for nothing, redeemed without price'
Further exchange: Rabbi Eliezer quotes 'if you return I will return'; Rabbi Yehoshua quotes God's ownership and forceful gathering
Final exchange: Rabbi Eliezer quotes 'through returning and rest you will be saved'; Rabbi Yehoshua quotes redemption despite despised state

topical_tags.term: Computing the End of Days / Kitzin
